title: Female employment rate, 1980 vs. 2024
subtitle: Share of the working-age population who are employed. Figures refer to [national estimates](#dod:ilo-national-estimates) and may not be fully comparable across countries.
caption:
Female employment rate, 1980 vs. 2024

Share of the working-age population who are employed. Figures refer to national estimates and may not be fully comparable across countries.

This scatter plot compares the female employment rate in 1980 on the vertical axis against the rate in 2024 on the horizontal axis, with each circle representing a country sized by population. The data reveals a general upward trend, as most labeled nations sit above the diagonal line where 1980 and 2024 values would be equal. The Bahamas shows the highest 2024 rate, positioned near 90% on the horizontal axis, while maintaining a rate around 25% in 1980. Nigeria displays a high 2024 value near 80% and a 1980 value around 35%. In contrast, Malawi and Thailand exhibit high 1980 rates between 70% and 90%, with 2024 rates falling between 40% and 60%. A dense cluster of countries, including the United States, Brazil, and the United Kingdom, occupies the center of the chart, with 2024 rates ranging roughly from 50% to 60% and 1980 rates between 40% and 50%. Several Asian and European nations, such as Italy, Spain, and the Netherlands, show 2024 rates between 50% and 65% but started from lower bases between 20% and 40% in 1980. At the lower end, Pakistan and Bangladesh have 2024 rates between 20% and 40%, with 1980 rates below 15%. The legend categorizes these points by region, with colors distinguishing North America, South America, Africa, Europe, Asia, and Oceania. Employment is defined in line with the 13th ICLS and includes the production of goods for own use, such as subsistence farming.
